_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill to evaluate requests for information to determine if response information exists.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business question, balancing statistical rigor, time constraints, data availability, and audience comprehension capacity.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI AB domain. AB-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-T-011 (RC, cosine: 0.508), RM-S-005 (RM, cosine: 0.490).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.5/10 (raw 0.353). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [determine, evaluate, exists, information, requests, response] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.859 (P=0.873, R=0.844). P exceeds R by 0.029 — the DCWF element's 12 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Skill to evaluate requests for information to determine if response information" and "Ability to select the appropriate analytical method for a given business questio" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.522 > STS 0.353 (gap +0.168). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 6.5%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [determine, evaluate, exists, information, requests, response]. WIDAI terms: [ability, analytical, appropriate, audience, availability, balancing].

signals.detail: Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.168 with low STS 0.353. Candidate selection was likely driven by shared vocabulary rather than functional alignment. The functionally correct KSA may not have been in the top-5 candidates.
